title: Level 900

Just could use more work by another author. |author=Pazlok |rewriter=Lambda, proof [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Message_Wall:Pazlok?threadId=4400000000000218655 here] |progress=Just started }} {{Foreground|File= Level 900 Glitch2.gif|Show= Toggle Glitch Gif ON (Epilepsy Warning)|Hide= Toggle Glitch Gif OFF}}
{{Class 2|entity= Presence of One Entity|color-s= 0%}} Level 900 is the 901st Level of the Backrooms. =
Description
= {{Underline|ffffff}}
{{Blurimage|file= Level 900 Castle.jpg|caption= Purgatory.|hovertext= What exists? What doesn't? Do I even exist?}}
Level 900, also known as "Requiem," takes the form of an expansive, presumably infinite castle with corridors that contain non-Euclidean geometry. The walls are made up of grey cobblestone and are made of wood on the inside. The floors are made up of fine, white marble and tend to erase all traces of dirt on it. The geometry inside Level 900 is unstable and changes at random intervals, which has occasionally led to wanderers getting stuck inside of the walls. Level 900 is shrouded in a "glitched" filter, making it look as though it was pixelated. When one exits, however, this filter still clings to the wanderer's vision. If one is exposed to the filter for too long before exiting the level, they will experience a large increase in paranoia. There is also a slight monotone filter surrounding the level, though this filter doesn't stick to the wanderer after one exits, and only seldom appears. There are no sources of vegetation inside the level and there are no objects that can be found inside. There are no immediate dangers inside Level 900. Despite this, however, one may experience certain thoughts that could lead to them feeling significantly different once they exit the level. The exact train of thoughts can vary from person to person, but one thing remains the same: They will always believe nobody around them matters. These thoughts occur randomly and may not occur at all, but it is advised that as soon as one feels these thoughts that they attempt to spite them. There is only one constant danger inside Level 900; That being the entities found inside. =
Entities
= {{Underline|ffffff}} {{Entity Class|no= (UND)|Hab= Level 900|Agg= 4|Int= 5|Dan= 5}} Flair is an entity commonly found inside of Level 900. It takes the form of a large, tripod-esque creature with a dome head. Its exact height is unknown, as it cannot be measured due to its hostility. It randomly wanders around the level. However, when it spots a wanderer, it immediately engages in attack. Its attacks consist of both long-range and close-range fighting styles. Any who are near the entity will start to believe many thoughts at once. The exact the wanderer will think are always random. Survivors of Flair's attacks have described feeling a sense of calm as the entity approached them, while others have described a sense of loss and despair. =
Colonies and Outposts
= {{Underline|ffffff}} Due to its extreme nature, no colonies lie within Level 900. =
Entrances and Exits
= {{Underline|ffffff}} ==
Entrances
== All reports of entrances to Level 900 are unconfirmed and there does not appear to be any consistent entry points. However, there have been multiple reports of random no-clipping into the level, all of which have been confirmed. ==
Exits
==
There are no exits.
{| class="fandom-table" |'''From:''' oramsay@meg.bri |- |'''To:''' jkevv@meg.bri |- |'''Subject:''' Regarding Level 900 |}

Hey, Jeremy, just wanted to talk. So, about Level 900, what's so special about it? I mean, it's just an everyday level that you can't escape but with a level-exclusive entity. We shouldn't even bother exploring it. Just leave it be. The only "special" thing about it is the whole "it being Level 900" thing. And that's not very special. So, what's the deal? Or are you just obsessed with it? I would appreciate it if you could get back to me as soon as possible. ---- {| class="fandom-table" |'''From:''' jkevv@meg.bri |- |'''To:''' oramsay@meg.bri |- |'''Subject:''' Regarding Level 900 |}

Here you go.
'''> Open File: level-900-draft-2.html'''
🗕 🗗 [[Level List|🗙]]
Waiting Enter message > I need help. Sorry, but I cannot properly provide you with help if you do not specify what you need help with. > I was assigned to Level 900, but everyone I talk to doesn't seem to recall any information about it. Can you tell them about it? Sorry, but I cannot assist with the exploration of levels that to not exist. > But I was assigned to it, so it must exist. Level 900 does not exist. > And just how exactly is that possible? Level 900 makes us believe it exists, when in reality, it doesn't. > So that means it exists? It would have to exist in order for it to trick us. But it doesn't. > How does that work? The non-existent Level 900 shares a "bond" with the realm of the existing. > This doesn't make any sense. Think of it like a thought in your head. That thought doesn't exist in real life. You can't interact with it. > I think I get it now, but what exactly does that mean? It means that it doesn't exist. The only part of it that could be considered "existing" is the fact that it tricks us into thinking that it is real. But even that is an illusion. Level 900 truly doesn't exist. It is merely a thought in our heads. It doesn't trick us, we only think that it does. > /quit Shutting down
- Level 900 has been updated. Click to view. -
{{Class 0|hexagon=Ø|color-h=0|color-s=0%|color-l=0%|class=N/A|safety=Non-existent|security=Devoid of Matter|entity=Unfit for Life}} Level 900 is the 901st level of the Backrooms. However, it does not exist. ==Description== Level 900 of the Backrooms does not exist. It tricks us into thinking that it exists, when, in reality, it doesn't. This phenomenon can be described as a "bond" shared by the realm of existence and non-existence. Level 900's original documentation was produced by a person under the influence of a phenomenon that made others create an official document about levels/entities/etc. that did not exist. This phenomenon has only ever occurred once, thus designating it as an "Unexplained Phenomenon". ==Entities== Aforementioned, Level 900 does not exist. Thus, no entities could inhabit it. Level 900 made the original document contain entities that didn't exist. ==Colonies and Outposts== Due to the fact that Level 900 doesn't exist, no colonies can be set up. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Level 900 cannot be entered because it does not exist. ===Exits=== *Don't obey the virus... Following an E-mail from Orion Ramsay to Jeremy Kevv, the documentation of the true Level 900 was left to a member of [[The Database]], though that person would later commit suicide due to unknown causes. Before this, however, they sent a message to another Database member. It reads as follows:
My dear friends, I don't have much time left. If anyone would like to take over my role, you are free to do so. I won't retaliate. Level 900 has left me in quite a conundrum. I can no longer decipher the difference between what is real and what is fake. The world is a swirl of black and white. Anyone who would like to add information about the level is free to do so. Just whatever you do, don't obey the virus.
> Open Attached File?
> Close Attached File?
Level 900 is itself an entity. It is trying to get us to document it so people will believe in its existence. If its current article is ever altered to show that it exists, change it back immediately. The more people that believe it exists, the more it grows and the more power it gains. We do not know what Level 900 looks like, and we may not know for the next million years. However, despite our efforts, it will emerge. It will find its way out of non-existence and possibly destroy the Backrooms as a whole. We can only delay it. But we must try all we can.
